studies.title: Dzarvis — multi-agent assistant on Claude
studies.teaser: Architecture write-up for Dzarvis: a multi-agent harness with narrow specialized subagents on top of a 208-tool MCP server. In stealth, fine-tuning with a focus group of 15 companies.

sections.heading: What Dzarvis is
sections.body: A multi-agent assistant on Claude. Daniel runs the orchestrator + a swarm of narrow specialized subagents over a 208-tool MCP server. Currently in stealth, fine-tuning with 15 companies.

sections.heading: Why a 208-tool MCP server
sections.body: Each tool is a contract: name + Zod-validated args + return shape. The orchestrator picks tools by name, the subagents are scoped to a narrow tool subset. This pushes routing logic into the type system rather than into hand-rolled if/else.

sections.heading: Why narrow subagents
sections.body: A single mega-prompt with all 208 tools available to one agent collapses under context-window pressure and tool-selection noise. Narrow subagents get tighter prompts, smaller tool surfaces, and predictable failure modes.

sections.heading: Trust boundary
sections.body: All LLM-emitted values that touch persistence go through Zod parsing first. URLs hit an allowlist before fetch. The system prompt explicitly tells the model to refuse with a structured error envelope when input is unparseable.
